1.14: Firm may act if the person dealing with the transaction or an immediate family member is the seller.
1.15: Firm may act if the person dealing with the transaction or an immediate family member is the borrower.
3.1.3: Solicitor must provide name and address of the seller's legal firm.
Lender's and borrower's legal advisers must verify the seller's firm is currently registered with the Law Society or Society of Licensed Conveyancers.
Lender must be notified immediately if the seller changes legal representation before completion.
3.1.4: Refer to contact details in section 1.7 if the seller does not have legal representation.
3.1.5: Accepted Identity Documents: Current passport, EEA member state photo ID card, EEA military or government documentation, UK photo card driving licence, UK paper driving licence (full), Firearms or Shotgun Certificate.
Accepted Address Documents (under 3 months old): Gas, electricity, or landline telephone bill; UK-based bank, building society, or credit card statement.

Accepted Address Documents (under 12 months old): Local Council or Housing Association tenancy agreement; non-MHBS mortgage statement; water bill; council tax bill; TV licence renewal; government department notification (e.g., DWP); HMRC tax notice.
3.2.3: Lender requires notification of the seller's solicitor's name and address.
3.2.4: Refer to contact details in section 1.7 if the seller has no legal representation.
4.1: Valuation reports are not provided if an Automated Valuation Model (AVM) report was utilized.
4.3: Refer to section 1.7 if valuation assumptions are incorrect.
4.5a: Refer to section 1.7 for re-inspection queries.
4.5b: Certificate of title must be sent to the contact address in section 1.7.
5.1.1: Refer to section 1.7 if the seller has owned the property for less than 6 months.
5.4.4: Environmental or contaminated land reports are not required.
5.4.6: Search insurance is only acceptable for remortgages or 're-bridge' transactions.
Insurance must cover the full loan amount and protect the mortgagee, successors, and assigns without limitation.
Solicitors must provide an unqualified Certificate of Title.
5.5.3a: Refer to section 1.7 if breach evidence exists or conditions will not be met by completion.
5.5.3b: Lender requires original or copy of planning permission.
5.5.3c: Lender requires original or copy of building regulation consents.
5.5.3d: Lender requires certificates of lawful use, development, or established use certificates.
5.5.4: Refer to section 1.7 if property is subject to restrictions affecting value or marketability.
5.7.1a: Lender must be contacted via section 1.7 details regarding flying freeholds.
5.7.1c: Refer to section 1.7 for reporting freehold flats or flying freeholds.
5.8.1: Security is accepted for buildings converted into up to four flats if the borrower occupies one, owns the freehold, and other flats are on long leases.
5.8.5: Security is not accepted where two leaseholders own each other's freehold reversions.
5.10.1: Refer to section 1.7 for restrictions on use.
5.13.1: Refer to section 1.7 if borrower is not providing the full balance or proposes a second charge.
5.14.1: Minimum unexpired lease term must be the mortgage term plus 50 years.
5.14.9: Initial ground rent capped at £250 per annum outside London or £1,000 per annum inside London; excesses must be reported.
Escalation linked to RPI or similar index is acceptable without notification.
Unreasonable multipliers (e.g., doubling every 5, 10, or 15 years) are not permitted without Society referral.
5.14.10: Refer to section 1.7 for service charge matters.
5.14.11: Indemnity insurance is not accepted for unsatisfactory lease terms.
5.14.12: Lender requires clear ground rent or service charge receipts.
5.14.13: Lender requires a receipted copy of notice or evidence of service.
5.14.15a: Refer to section 1.7 regarding absentee or insolvent landlords.
5.14.15b: Indemnity insurance is not accepted for absent or insolvent landlords.
5.15.2a: Refer to section 1.7 for management company issues.
5.15.2b: Management company share certificate must be sent to the lender.
5.15.2c: Signed blank stock transfer form must be sent to the lender.
5.15.2d: Management company memorandum and articles of association must be sent to the lender.
5.16.2: Refer to section 1.7 if unable to certify that a search entry does not relate to the property.
5.17.5a: Power of attorney must be sent to the lender.
5.17.5b: Statutory declaration of non-revocation of power of attorney must be sent to the lender.
5.19.1: Refer to section 1.7 for affordable housing, shared equity, or shared ownership queries.
5.20.1: Report to lender if lease fails to meet UK Finance minimum requirements for solar PV panels on roof space.
5.20.3: Lease must contain a valid mortgagee break right exercisable upon repossession.
Leasehold properties require landlord consent for equipment installation.
Borrower must confirm their buildings insurance company has been notified of the installation.
5.20.4: Disclosure of existing Green Deal Plans is mandatory.

6.6.3: Lender requires a counterpart or certified copy of the tenancy agreement.
6.6.4: Lending on Houses in Multiple Occupation (HMO) is not permitted.
6.7.1: Acceptable warranty schemes: NHBC, Premier Guarantee, Building Life Plan, LABC New Home Warranty, or Architect’s/Chartered Surveyor’s Certificate.
Properties under 10 years old without these schemes limited to 33.33% LTV, subject to building survey and criteria.
Other providers require approval from the Head of Mortgages or Credit Committee.
6.7.2: Warranty certificate must be sent to the lender.
6.7.3: Assignments of building standards indemnity schemes must be sent to the lender.
6.7.4: Lender will not proceed if the property lacks a new home warranty scheme unless exceptions in 6.7.1 are met.
6.7.6: Professional consultant's certificate must be sent to the lender.
